mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 10% signals a tightening labor market, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60% or more of instances where UNRATE falls below 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, which enhances corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive 5-day returns in 50% or more of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during a demand-driven recovery phase indicates robust economic activity, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 60% or more of instances where GDPC1 shows growth, or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts (HOUST) level above 1,200 thousand units signals strong demand for residential construction, which boosts related industries and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60% or more of instances where HOUST exceeds 1,200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals central bank intervention through rate hikes, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60% or more of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer and residential demand, which drives corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to produce positive 5-day returns in 50% or more of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal higher borrowing costs, which dampen corporate investment and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60% or more of instances where FEDFUNDS rises, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
